title: Giovanni Pico della Mirandola
summary: Prodigy, count, disputant, theologian-philosopher, and unstable emblem of Renaissance dignity. The portal treats Pico as a figure whose short life moved between university scholasticism, Florentine Platonism, Hebrew/Kabbalistic study, papal trouble, and late Savonarolan religion.

title: Christian Kabbalah in Pico
subtitle: Mithridates, Hebrew, 900 Conclusions
summary: Pico's Kabbalah depends on translation, Hebrew aspiration, Christian interpretation, and the ambitious integration of Jewish mystical materials into concordist philosophy.

title: Pico and Astrology
subtitle: From scientia naturalis to Disputationes
summary: Pico's anti-astrology polemic must be studied as a development from earlier natural philosophy and celestial/Kabbalistic interests into late Florentine anti-divinatory critique.

title: Against the Oration-Only Pico
subtitle: Copenhaver, Farmer, Howlett, Walden
summary: The portal's governing historiographical correction: Pico cannot be reduced to a proto-modern Oration on dignity. The Oration must be read as preface, reception object, mystical text, and part of the 900 Conclusions crisis.

title: The Anti-Pico Problem
subtitle: Textual tampering and late doctrine
summary: Farmer's textual-transmission argument warns that late printed Pico, especially the Disputationes, cannot be used doctrinally without checking Gianfrancesco/San Marco risk.

title: Brian P. Copenhaver
subtitle: Renaissance philosophy, magic, Pico reception
summary: Copenhaver is a central modern interpreter for this portal because his corpus here ties Pico's Oration to magic, Kabbalah, modern memory, and the scholastic-juridical details of the Roman heresy case.

title: Chaim Wirszubski
subtitle: Pico and Jewish mysticism
summary: Wirszubski clarifies the sources, manuscripts, translations, and Christianizing shape of Pico's Kabbalah, making him essential for any serious portal treatment of Pico's Hebrew and Kabbalistic materials.

title: Ovanes Akopyan
subtitle: Pico and astrology
summary: Akopyan supplies the main structure for reading Pico's Disputationes, its prehistory, sources, natural philosophy, and pro/contra afterlife.

title: Sophia Howlett
subtitle: Aristotelianism, Kabbalism, Platonism
summary: Howlett re-enters Pico studies from Ficino studies and frames Pico as a contested site whose allure depends on the interplay of exceptionalism, Kabbalah, Aristotelianism, Platonism, and modern scholarly shifts away from an Oration-only Pico.

title: Stephen A. Farmer
subtitle: 900 Theses and syncretic systems
summary: Farmer restores the 900 Conclusions as a massive puzzle and oral-disputational project whose hidden connections structure Pico's Roman ambition.

title: Section Summary Style Guide
subtitle: Arguments, references, coverage
summary: PicoDB now has a formal style guide requiring every section summary to reconstruct argument moves, register references, extract claims, mark uncertainties, and name knowledge-product hooks.

title: Summary Coverage Protocol
subtitle: UNREAD to PROMOTED
summary: The reading system now tracks coverage levels from unread catalog entries through source packets, section summaries, claims, cross-links, review, and website promotion.

title: 900 Conclusions
subtitle: Concordist disputation program
summary: The 900 Conclusions are the structural engine behind Pico's Roman crisis: a massive public program of philosophical and theological theses whose condemned subset produced the trial archive.

title: 900 Conclusions as a Knowledge System
subtitle: Thesis clusters and hidden connections
summary: The 900 Conclusions should be treated as a debate database of compressed propositions, cross-links, traditions, and edition problems.

title: Disputationes adversus astrologiam divinatricem
subtitle: Anti-astrology and late Savonarolan proximity
summary: The anti-astrology work belongs to Pico's late Florentine years and is central for mapping the divergence from Ficino and the shared terrain with Savonarola.

title: Heptaplus
subtitle: Genesis, creation, biblical hermeneutics
summary: Heptaplus shifts Pico away from a simplified secular dignity myth and toward biblical exegesis, creation theology, and the problem of posthumous textual transmission.

title: Heptaplus: Genesis and the Four Worlds
subtitle: Sevenfold Mosaic interpretation
summary: The Heptaplus reads Genesis as hidden natural and divine philosophy, organized by coherent sevenfold interpretation and a four-world cosmology.

title: On Being and the One
subtitle: Concord and apophasis
summary: Pico reconciles Plato and Aristotle by distinguishing senses of being and by moving from semantics to Dionysian divine darkness.

title: Oration on the Dignity of Man
subtitle: Roman disputation preface turned modern emblem
summary: The Oration must be treated both as a text attached to the failed 900 Theses debate and as a later object of modern memory, humanist canonization, and debate over dignity, freedom, magic, and Kabbalah.

title: Oration: Indeterminacy and Ascent
subtitle: Opening sequence
summary: The Oration's famous opening is a disciplined program of self-shaping, angelic imitation, philosophical purgation, and ascent to union with God.
